message:
feat(indexer): Card 1 parallel-WAL shadow observer + IndexRunLockHeartbeat/FTS repair perf fixes, with A/B harness

Five intertwined changes surfaced by the Cards 1/2/3 profiling run;
splitting them would break bisectability because the bench harness
exists specifically to measure the three behavioral changes together.

1. NEW: src/indexer/parallel_wal_shadow.rs (~405 lines)
   Parallel-WAL shadow observer per
   `tests/artifacts/perf/2026-04-21-profile-run/ALIEN-ARTIFACT-CARD1-SPEC.md §5.7`.
   Does NOT change commit semantics — we run the existing
   `persist_conversations_batched_begin_concurrent` path unchanged
   but instrument it to record:
     * when each writer chunk starts/ends and its wall-micros
     * "would-have-coalesced" boundaries where a real parallel-WAL
       coordinator would have issued one combined epoch fsync
       instead of N
   Aggregated as `ParallelWalShadowTelemetry`, exposed via
   `cass health --json` under `responsiveness.parallel_wal_shadow`.
   Activation:
     (unset)                          → shadow ON (default post-flip)
     CASS_INDEXER_PARALLEL_WAL=shadow → same
     CASS_INDEXER_PARALLEL_WAL=off    → disabled (zero overhead)
     any other value including `on`/`commit` → rejected; commit
       path deliberately not exposed until 100+ consecutive shadow
       runs show stable numbers
   Module-internal (`pub(crate)`) so the only external surface is
   the telemetry record.

2. src/indexer/mod.rs — IndexRunLockHeartbeat shutdown
   Swap `thread::sleep(interval)` → `thread::park_timeout(interval)`
   inside the heartbeat worker, and in `Drop` call
   `join.thread().unpark()` after `stop.store(true, Relaxed)` so
   the worker wakes on next stop-flag read. With the old `sleep`,
   every `run_index` paid up to `interval` (default 1 s) at
   shutdown while the main thread blocked on `join()` — which
   *quantized bench wall-clock to 1 s bins regardless of the
   actual indexing cost*. `park_timeout` preserves the periodic-
   heartbeat semantics (returns on token / deadline / spuriously;
   outer `while` re-checks `stop`) but makes the shutdown path
   O(drain), not O(interval).

3. src/indexer/mod.rs — repair_fallback_fts_after_full_index_run
   Previously used the caller's long-running `FrankenStorage`
   connection. The rayon worker pool's cursor-cancellation
   bookkeeping (`observe_cursor_cancellation` in fsqlite-btree)
   accumulates per-thread state that surfaces as `SQLITE_ABORT`
   ("callback requested query abort") on the FIRST subsequent
   query — which was making every full-rebuild `run_index` call
   in the bench harness Err after ~1 s of abort bookkeeping,
   swamping the actual indexing wall-clock.
   Fix: open a short-lived, fresh `FrankenStorage` (10 s open
   timeout) for every step of the repair — fingerprint probe,
   consistency check, marker write. Caller's `_storage` is
   intentionally NOT consulted; it's kept on the signature for
   API stability and marked `_storage` so the compiler flags
   any accidental use. Repair work is bounded (one `COUNT(*)`
   probe, optional batched rebuild, single meta write) and
   strictly idempotent on disk state.

4. src/indexer/mod.rs — persist + streaming-consumer wiring
   Plumb the shadow observer through
   `persist_conversations_batched_begin_concurrent` via the new
   `parallel_wal_shadow::start_chunk(...)` guard in the chunk
   loop, and add a companion telemetry field on the streaming
   consumer so per-message vs combine paths record shadow timings
   consistently. Persist-mod changes are mechanical wiring + a
   new `pub fn` surface for test-only fixtures; no commit path
   touched.

5. NEW benches/index_perf.rs::bench_card_defaults_ab and
   NEW tests/card_defaults_wallclock_ab.rs (~258 lines)
   Measured A/B of the post-flip defaults (Cards 1/2/3 all on)
   vs the pre-flip "legacy" config (static governor, per-message
   consumer, shadow observer off), plus the two middle corners so
   per-card attribution is possible:
     legacy_all_off       (static, combine=0, shadow=off)
     new_all_on           (conformal, combine=1, shadow=shadow)
     only_combine_on      (static, combine=1, shadow=off)
     only_governor_on     (conformal, combine=0, shadow=off)
   Corpus size 200, `--force-rebuild` so wall-clock includes
   the full scan+persist+Tantivy index path; sample_size=10.
   The `card_defaults_wallclock_ab` integration test is a
   direct-test variant of the same matrix: Criterion's framework +
   the sibling build.rs path kept leaving orphaned bench binaries
   pinned at 97% CPU on a shared 128-core box, so the test
   measures wall-clock once per cell without Criterion driving.
   Runs with RAYON_NUM_THREADS capped, corpus of unique-tokened
   ~2 KiB message bodies to grow the Tantivy postings list the
   same way real corpora do.

6. src/indexer/mod.rs — new #[cfg(test)] coverage
   ~400 lines of new tests, including:
     parallel_wal_shadow_observer_does_not_change_persisted_state
     parallel_wal_shadow_observer_emits_chunk_telemetry
   that pin the invariant "shadow is pure observation — the
   persisted SQLite + Tantivy state after a shadow run equals the
   persisted state with the shadow disabled".

All three behavioral changes (governor default flip, consumer
combine, shadow observer) are individually opt-out via env vars;
the default is every card ON.
